name: First Era — The Foundation
description: Civilizations forming, gods establishing. The defining tensions of the First Era branch into multiple possible Second Era states.

axes.name: The Southern Offer
axes.description: Trade vs isolation. The Sunward Courts propose a trade route through the mountain passes. Thornwall must decide.

outcomes.name: Trade Opened Freely
outcomes.description: Southern goods flow north, northern ore flows south. Thornwall gets a southern quarter. Young smiths read about metallurgy. Old masters grieve.
outcomes.second_era_result: Blended northern culture emerges — literate, trade-connected, prosperous. But the True Forge retreats to the deep mountains, keeping old ways. Two northern cultures where there was one.

outcomes.name: Trade Rejected
outcomes.description: Thornwall closed the passes. The Senate built a trade post at the mountain's base anyway. Young smiths slip down at night. A black market forms.
outcomes.second_era_result: Thornwall intact but brittle. The Foot — a town of southern traders and northern dissidents — grows at the mountain's base. The Forge Council pretends it doesn't exist.

outcomes.name: Trade Negotiated
outcomes.description: The seventeen-clause treaty: ore goes south, medicine comes north, nobody enters the forges. Holds for a generation, then a mixed-culture child dissolves the walls.
outcomes.second_era_result: Gradual blending nobody planned. The treaty technically still in effect. Nobody enforces it. A new generation speaks both languages and finds old arguments tiresome. Most stable outcome.

outcomes.name: No Player Engagement
outcomes.description: NPC-decided compromise pleased nobody. Trade exists but is resentful. Both sides feel cheated.
outcomes.second_era_result: Simmering tension. Neither open nor closed. The costs of trade without the trust, the isolation without the purity.

axes.name: The Ash Cough / Ashfield Awakening
axes.description: A plague born from accumulated fear. The Ashfield god breathes plague without malice. How the crisis is addressed shapes theology, medicine, and the nature of divinity.

outcomes.name: Treated (Bond Healers)
outcomes.description: Bond-attuned healers drain the fear. Death rate drops from 1-in-3 to 1-in-20. Treatment, not cure. The healers suffer — the work damages them.
outcomes.second_era_result: Endemic disease. Society adapts. Fear-drainers become a recognized profession and a political force. The Ashfield god continues growing slowly. Problem managed, not solved.

outcomes.name: Investigated (Ashfield Explored)
outcomes.description: The player discovers the god is a newborn breathing plague without malice. Knowledge spreads unevenly. Scholars debate. The Ash Tenders say they knew.
outcomes.second_era_result: Intellectual crisis. If the god isn't malicious, is killing it murder? If the plague is breath, is quarantine imprisonment? The philosophical divide deepens.

outcomes.name: Quarantined
outcomes.description: Thornwall sealed. The infected died isolated. The healthy survived behind walls. Trust between the forge quarter and upper town shattered.
outcomes.second_era_result: Thornwall physically survived but socially fractured. The upper town and forge quarter are effectively separate communities. Walls built for plague still standing.

outcomes.name: Rootwalker Knowledge
outcomes.description: An elder sings the old songs. The Ashfield wasn't always feared. The concept of unworship enters the conversation: change what people believe, change the god.
outcomes.second_era_result: Radical theological movement. If worship creates gods, can unworship unmake them? The Ash Tenders and Rootwalker elders form an unlikely alliance. The Senate wants to study it.

outcomes.name: No Player Engagement
outcomes.description: Nobody investigates. Nobody discovers unworship. The plague becomes background. Cultures adapt around it.
outcomes.second_era_result: Stage 2 by year 20. Stage 3 by year 40. Stage 4 by year 60. The world gets older without getting wiser.

axes.name: The Three Answers
axes.description: Three philosophies emerge from the crisis. Each has genuine value AND serves someone's power. They harden from ideas into political identities.

outcomes.name: Iron Doctrine Dominant
outcomes.description: You are what you make. If you don't make things, your opinions are worth less. Serves the Forge Council's authority — but produces genuine artisans.
outcomes.second_era_result: Northern conservatism. Craft as moral framework. The Forge Council's philosophy becomes the dominant culture. Prosperity through making, rigidity through certainty.

outcomes.name: Consul's Proposition Dominant
outcomes.description: We understand the problem, therefore we should lead the solution. Serves the Senate's expansion — but produces genuine scholars.
outcomes.second_era_result: Senate interventionism. Knowledge-as-authority fills the vacuum. Genuine advances in medicine, engineering, law. But authority without trust is just control.

outcomes.name: Song of the Cracked Stone
outcomes.description: You cannot own the river. The settled world's problems come from settling. Serves Rootwalker identity — but produces genuine wisdom about adaptability.
outcomes.second_era_result: Rootwalker resistance philosophy spreads. Young people leave settled communities. The Song becomes an anthem. What happens when a generation decides old structures aren't worth maintaining?

outcomes.name: No Dominant Philosophy
outcomes.description: No philosophy wins. They become identity markers instead of living ideas. People stop asking 'what's the answer?' and start asking 'which side are you on?'
outcomes.second_era_result: Philosophical stagnation. Three camps, no synthesis. Ideas die when they become team jerseys.

notable_combinations.name: Prosperous Conservatism
notable_combinations.summary: A prosperous but conservative north. Trade enriches everyone but the old masters feel sold out. The True Forge in the deep mountains becomes a symbol of resistance. Managed plague creates permanent healthcare dependency. Tension: prosperity vs identity.

notable_combinations.name: Radical Isolation
notable_combinations.summary: An isolated but philosophically radical north. The Foot becomes the most interesting place in the world — where ideas cross borders even if goods don't. The unworship movement creates the first serious challenge to how gods work. Tension: can you change the nature of the divine?

notable_combinations.name: Ordered Scars
notable_combinations.summary: Structurally stable but emotionally scarred. The treaty holds. The quarantine walls hold. Everything holds. But trust is gone. The Senate's knowledge-as-authority philosophy fills the vacuum. Order without trust is just control.

notable_combinations.name: The Default World
notable_combinations.summary: Nobody engaged. Resentful compromises. Endemic problems. Stagnant cultures. Not apocalyptic — just less. A world that got older without getting wiser. The heaviest ending.

emerging_cultures.name: The True Forge
emerging_cultures.description: Iron Holds purists who rejected trade. Oral tradition only. No outsiders. The most skilled smiths in the world, making weapons for nobody.

emerging_cultures.name: Bridge Folk
emerging_cultures.description: Mixed-culture children of the trade quarter. Speak both languages. Belong to both worlds and neither.

emerging_cultures.name: The Foot
emerging_cultures.description: The unofficial town at the mountain's base. Half southern traders, half northern dissidents. A culture of defiance and commerce.

emerging_cultures.name: The Fear-Drainers' Guild
emerging_cultures.description: Bond-attuned healers who specialize in Ash Cough. A profession that becomes a political force.

emerging_cultures.name: The Unworshippers
emerging_cultures.description: Can you change a god by changing the story? Part scholar, part heretic, part revolutionary.

emerging_cultures.name: The Delta Syndicate
emerging_cultures.description: The Brackish evolving into a proper mercantile power as trade increases.

fates.song_dominant: Legendary figure. Her songs define a generation.
fates.default: Dies in obscurity. But the Rootwalkers remember her songs.

fates.trade_open: Dies vindicated — his craft lives on in the trade.
fates.trade_rejected: Dies bitter — his forge goes to nobody who meets his standard.
Pivoted to other strategies. She always lands on her feet.

fates.plague_rootwalker: Critical figure. Was she right all along?
fates.default: Eccentric elder, talking to steam. Right about everything, heard by nobody.
